flow_overview: A park-specific amusement park pass purchasing flow that lets users choose a Six Flags park, browse season pass options, configure add-ons, enter guest information, and select a payment plan during checkout.
capability_summary: Park selection and park-specific context switching
Flexible payment plan selection and agreement handling

requirements.text: The system shall allow users to select a specific park before entering a park-specific pass purchase flow.
requirements.rationale: The flow begins with a park selection experience and then shows Great Escape selected, which directly grounds park choice as an entry step for the later purchase flow.

requirements.text: The system shall switch the user into park-specific browsing context after a park is selected.
requirements.rationale: After Great Escape is chosen, the next screen shows Great Escape branding and park-specific content, indicating a context change into the chosen park experience.

requirements.text: The system shall provide a park-specific entry point for discovering available pass options.
requirements.rationale: The park page surfaces pass-related content and then leads into a dedicated Tickets and Passes page, grounding pass discovery as a supported action from the selected park context.

requirements.text: The system shall present season pass tiers side by side with pricing and benefit information to support plan comparison.
requirements.rationale: The pass page shows multiple pass cards together, each with price and listed benefits, which directly supports comparison across available tiers.

requirements.text: The system shall allow the user to initiate purchase of a specific pass tier from the comparison view.
requirements.rationale: The Diamond pass is available as a selectable option in the comparison view, and the next screen shows Diamond pass purchase configuration, which grounds pass-specific purchase initiation.

requirements.text: The system shall allow users to configure the quantity of passes being purchased.
requirements.rationale: The Diamond pass configuration screen includes quantity controls next to the selected pass, which directly suggests quantity configuration even though only one quantity state is shown.

requirements.text: The system shall allow each purchased pass to be assigned to a named guest or passholder during checkout.
requirements.rationale: The checkout flow shows a guest or passholder card and then populates it with the entered person name, grounding pass assignment to an individual.

requirements.text: The system shall collect personal information needed for passholder setup during checkout.
requirements.rationale: The flow clearly shows a personal information form being filled across several steps, including fields such as name, email, zip code, and age, which grounds structured passholder data capture.

requirements.text: The system shall preserve earlier user-entered personal information while the user completes the remaining required fields.
requirements.rationale: Across successive form states, previously entered values remain visible while additional fields are filled, which directly grounds cross-step retention within the form.

requirements.text: The system shall support copying previously entered passholder information into billing-related information where appropriate.
requirements.rationale: The form exposes a Copy info to billing control, the control becomes selected, and a later agreement screen shows billing-related information, which supports a grounded but not fully end-to-end inference about data reuse.

requirements.text: The system shall collect age or date-of-birth information when needed for pass purchase or financing eligibility.
requirements.rationale: An age field appears in the personal information step, and the later agreement step references date of birth, which grounds age-related data collection even though the underlying business rule is not fully visible.

requirements.text: The system shall keep the selected pass and passholder context visible and consistent across later checkout stages.
requirements.rationale: Later checkout screens continue to show the chosen Diamond pass and named guest, which directly grounds cross-step state consistency and context preservation.

requirements.text: The system shall allow users to configure optional dining-plan add-ons as part of a season pass purchase.
requirements.rationale: The checkout flow contains a dedicated add-on step for dining plans with selectable options, directly grounding add-on configuration within the purchase journey.

requirements.text: The system shall present multiple dining-plan variants with pricing so the user can compare optional add-ons.
requirements.rationale: The add-on screen shows One Meal Dining Plan and Two Meal Dining Plan side by side with price information, directly supporting comparison among add-on variants.

requirements.text: The system shall provide visible feedback when an add-on selection becomes active.
requirements.rationale: The dining-plan step changes from an unselected to a selected state, which grounds the expectation of explicit UI feedback for active add-on choices.

requirements.text: The system shall offer a choice between full payment and a flexible installment payment plan for pass purchases.
requirements.rationale: The payment step explicitly presents two selectable options, one for flexible pay and one for paying in full, which directly grounds payment-method choice.

requirements.text: The system shall display the installment schedule and payment amounts when a flexible payment plan is offered.
requirements.rationale: The flexible pay screen includes a dated payment schedule with amounts and totals, which directly grounds schedule transparency even though actual billing execution is not visible.

requirements.text: The system shall provide visible feedback when the chosen payment plan becomes the active selection.
requirements.rationale: The flexible pay option changes from unselected to selected between the two payment-plan states, directly grounding explicit selection feedback for financing choice.

requirements.text: The system shall support stepwise progression through the checkout flow with consistent back and continue navigation controls.
requirements.rationale: Multiple checkout stages show repeated back and continue or next controls, which grounds structured progression support across the purchase workflow.

requirements.text: The system shall require the user to review and consent to financing-related terms before completing a flexible payment agreement.
requirements.rationale: A terms and conditions modal explicitly instructs the user to review the agreement, sign, and consent, which directly grounds agreement review as part of financing completion.

requirements.text: The system shall collect billing and contact details needed to complete the payment agreement workflow.
requirements.rationale: Behind the terms modal, the agreement screen visibly includes buyer, mailing, contact, and date-of-birth related fields, which grounds billing and contact data capture for the agreement step.

requirements.text: The system shall present prices and option details transparently enough for users to compare pass tiers, add-ons, and payment choices during checkout.
requirements.rationale: The flow repeatedly exposes prices, monthly payment messaging, add-on costs, and financing amounts across several stages, which grounds a broader transparency-oriented requirement about decision support.

requirements.text: The system shall retain the selected park context in later purchase and checkout screens so users can verify that they are still completing a pass purchase for the intended park.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: partially_fulfilled
Mutation family: persistence_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-02
Source gold text: The system shall switch the user into park-specific browsing context after a park is selected.
Generation rationale: This extends the accepted park-specific context requirement into later checkout continuity. It stays close to the same flow but asks for stronger cross-screen persistence that may only be partly visible or inconsistently surfaced.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall switch the user into park-specific browsing context after a park is selected.

requirements.text: The system shall send a confirmation to the purchaser's contact address after checkout that summarizes the selected park, pass tier, add-ons, and payment arrangement.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: partially_fulfilled
Mutation family: external_effect_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-21
Source gold text: The system shall collect billing and contact details needed to complete the payment agreement workflow.
Generation rationale: The flow already collects contact information during a purchase workflow, so a downstream confirmation is a realistic nearby requirement. It is useful contrast because the visible UI may support the setup for it without proving the external delivery effect.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall collect billing and contact details needed to complete the payment agreement workflow.

requirements.text: The system shall visibly communicate when a flexible installment option is unavailable because the purchaser does not satisfy age or eligibility policy requirements.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: abstain
Mutation family: policy_or_role_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-11
Source gold text: The system shall collect age or date-of-birth information when needed for pass purchase or financing eligibility.
Generation rationale: The accepted gold set already ties age or date-of-birth capture to financing eligibility, so an explicit policy-based rejection or ineligibility message is a disciplined extension. It is realistic but depends on business rules that may not be observable in the provided flow.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall collect age or date-of-birth information when needed for pass purchase or financing eligibility.

requirements.text: The system shall perform any required financing or approval check before finalizing a flexible payment agreement and present the resulting status to the user.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: abstain
Mutation family: hidden_state_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-20
Source gold text: The system shall require the user to review and consent to financing-related terms before completing a flexible payment agreement.
Generation rationale: A financing-related agreement flow plausibly implies a backend approval or eligibility step, while the visible core would be a user-facing status or outcome. This is useful contrast because the backend decision itself is hidden even if some UI feedback may exist.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall require the user to review and consent to financing-related terms before completing a flexible payment agreement.

requirements.text: The system shall provide a final editable review step before commitment where the purchaser can inspect and revise the selected park, pass tier, passholder details, dining add-ons, and payment choice.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: not_fulfilled
Mutation family: missing_visible_step
Source gold requirement: REQ-19
Source gold text: The system shall support stepwise progression through the checkout flow with consistent back and continue navigation controls.
Generation rationale: A structured multi-step checkout often includes an explicit review-and-edit phase before commitment, especially when personal details, add-ons, and financing choices are involved. This stays tightly anchored to the existing flow but introduces a plausible visible step that may be absent.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall support stepwise progression through the checkout flow with consistent back and continue navigation controls.

requirements.text: The system shall keep the checkout pricing summary synchronized with user choices so that changing an add-on or payment plan visibly updates the relevant one-time and recurring charges.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: not_fulfilled
Mutation family: stronger_visible_constraint
Source gold requirement: REQ-17
Source gold text: The system shall display the installment schedule and payment amounts when a flexible payment plan is offered.
Generation rationale: The gold set already establishes visible prices, add-on offers, and flexible payment amounts, so synchronized recalculation is a realistic stronger expectation in the same checkout space. It can diversify later review because the flow may show pricing information without clearly showing dynamic updates across choice changes.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall display the installment schedule and payment amounts when a flexible payment plan is offered.
